Collignon.notes: Band based in the Netherlands and Portugal

Deedrah.notes: Deedrah is listed as an alias/project of French producer Frederic Holyszewski.

Domestic.notes: MusicBrainz identifies the psytrance project as from Tel Aviv-Jaffa, Israel.

Dubsahara Meets Vlastur.notes: Dubsahara is London UK; Vlastur is Greek

Frederika.notes: MusicBrainz disambiguation says French electronic music artist.

Gaudi.notes: MusicBrainz describes Gaudi as Anglo-Italian and lists begin area Bologna and area London.

Gaudi + Don Letts + Earl 16.notes: Listed collaboration; countries represent each named participant.

Gerő.notes: Matched MusicBrainz entry for Hungarian musician Gergely Nagy.

Gumi.notes: Matched downtempo artist Maor Hasbani; MusicBrainz area lists Tel Aviv.

Headroom.notes: Matched South African psytrance producer Adam Metcalfe.

Justin Chaos.notes: MusicBrainz begin area lists Argentina; area lists London.

Kalki.notes: Matched psytrance artist Thibault Fontaine; MusicBrainz area lists Lyon.

Koxbox / Saiko Pod.notes: Koxbox source identifies the Danish group and states Psychopod was renamed Saiko-Pod.

Marysia Osu & Kibir La Amlak.notes: Marysia Osu is Polish-born; Kibir La Amlak is London UK

My Baby.notes: Source describes My Baby as a Dutch-New Zealand band from Amsterdam.

Óperentzia.notes: Source describes Operentzia as a Hungarian dub-trance duo.

OTT.notes: Mapped as Ott the British psychedelic dub producer.

Platon Karataev Duo.notes: Mapped from Platon Karataev, sourced as a Budapest/Hungarian group.

Prometheus.notes: Source identifies Benji Vaughan, aka Prometheus, as an English psytrance producer.

Raja Ram & Lucas.notes: Australian-born Raja Ram and UK DJ Lucas

Shpongle.notes: Origin listed as Chobham, Surrey, England.

Star Sounds Orchestra.notes: Source describes German musician Steve Schroyder founding Star Sounds Orchestra with Jens Zygar.

sources: DJ Taku / Green Magic event profile

Tristan.notes: Source identifies Tristan Cooke as a British DJ and producer.

Tsuyoshi Suzuki.notes: Source identifies Joujouka as a Japanese project from Tokyo formed by Takeshi Isogai and Tsuyoshi Suzuki.

Undefined Behavior.notes: Collaboration of Swiss artist Ajja and Brazilian artist Burn In Noise

Vibrasphere.notes: Origin listed as Uppsala, Sweden and Brussels, Belgium.
